checklist_item.title: Does transaction validation reject nonce >= U64.MAX_VALUE?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Submit tx with nonce = U64.MAX_VALUE.
2. Verify rejection with NonceOverflowError.
3. Test comparison width (U256 vs U64).
4. Verify no wrap-around is possible.
5. Test at exact boundary.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0035 from PROP-W2B11-1, Trust Boundary: tb-001, tb-002

checklist_item.title: Does contract creation reject initcode > MAX_INIT_CODE_SIZE?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Submit contract creation with oversized initcode.
2. Verify rejection with InitCodeTooLargeError.
3. Test at exact MAX_INIT_CODE_SIZE boundary.
4. Verify check applies only when tx.to == Bytes0.
5. Test normal transactions with large data - should accept.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0036 from PROP-W2B11-1, Trust Boundary: tb-001, tb-002

checklist_item.title: Does transaction validation enforce EIP-7825 TX_MAX_GAS_LIMIT?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Submit tx with gas > 16777216.
2. Verify rejection with TransactionGasLimitExceededError.
3. Test boundary at exact 16777216.
4. Verify Fusaka-specific implementation.
5. Cross-check across client implementations.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0037 from PROP-W2B11-1, Trust Boundary: tb-001, tb-002

checklist_item.title: Is EIP-7623 token counting correct (zero=1, non-zero=4)?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Create tx with known calldata pattern.
2. Calculate expected token count manually.
3. Verify client produces same count.
4. Test with all-zero, all-nonzero, mixed calldata.
5. Verify Fusaka-specific implementation.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0038 from PROP-W2B11-1, Trust Boundary: tb-001

checklist_item.title: Is calldata_floor calculated correctly per EIP-7623?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Calculate tokens from calldata.
2. Verify floor = tokens * 10 + 21000.
3. Test with various calldata patterns.
4. Verify constants FLOOR_CALLDATA_COST=10, TX_BASE_COST=21000.
5. Verify Fusaka-specific implementation.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0039 from PROP-W2B11-1, Trust Boundary: tb-001

checklist_item.title: Is decode_transaction the inverse of encode_transaction?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Create valid transactions of each type.
2. Encode then decode.
3. Verify decode(encode(tx)) == tx.
4. Test with adversarial byte sequences.
5. Verify no silent misinterpretation.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0040 from PROP-W2B11-1, Trust Boundary: tb-004

checklist_item.title: Does decode_transaction reject unknown type prefixes?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Send bytes starting with 0x05, 0xFF, etc.
2. Verify TransactionTypeError is raised.
3. Verify no fallthrough to default behavior.
4. Test all invalid prefix bytes.
5. Verify no undefined state transitions.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0041 from PROP-W2B11-1, Trust Boundary: tb-004

checklist_item.title: Does decode_transaction safely handle malformed P2P bytes?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Fuzz decode_transaction with random bytes.
2. Verify only TransactionTypeError is raised.
3. No IndexError, OverflowError, MemoryError.
4. Verify no node crashes.
5. This is the outermost trust boundary.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0042 from PROP-W2B11-1, Trust Boundary: tb-004

checklist_item.title: Are EIP-7702 authorizations bound to chain_id and nonce?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Create EIP-7702 authorization with specific nonce.
2. Verify authorization only valid for that nonce.
3. Increment nonce and verify replay fails.
4. Verify chain_id is included in signing hash.
5. Test cross-chain replay prevention.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0043 from PROP-W2B11-1, Trust Boundary: tb-008

checklist_item.title: Is transaction hash computation deterministic and unique?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Compute hash for legacy tx: keccak256(rlp.encode(tx)).
2. Compute hash for typed tx: keccak256(type_prefix + rlp.encode(tx)).
3. Verify uniqueness across different transactions.
4. Verify determinism on repeated computation.
5. Check mempool deduplication correctness.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0044 from PROP-W2B11-1, Trust Boundary: tb-001

checklist_item.title: Does set_code trigger empty account destruction when appropriate?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Create account with only code (no balance, nonce=0).
2. Set code to empty via EIP-7702 revocation.
3. Verify account is destroyed.
4. No ghost accounts should persist.
5. Test modify_state cleanup integration.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0047 from PROP-W2B11-1, Trust Boundary: tb-003

checklist_item.title: Does EIP-155 signing hash include (chain_id, 0, 0) padding?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Create EIP-155 legacy transaction.
2. Verify signing hash = keccak256(rlp([..., chain_id, 0, 0])).
3. Verify trailing zeros are present.
4. Test cross-chain replay prevention.
5. Compare against non-155 signing hash.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-0048 from PROP-W2B11-1, Trust Boundary: tb-009

checklist_item.title: Does bytes_to_bls_field reject values >= BLS_MODULUS?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Create blob with field element encoding >= BLS_MODULUS.
2. Verify bytes_to_bls_field raises error.
3. Verify no silent modular reduction.
4. Test across multiple client implementations.
5. Verify consensus on rejection.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-1, Trust Boundary: tb-001

checklist_item.title: Are cell_to_coset_evals and coset_evals_to_cell exact inverses?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Generate random valid CosetEvals.
2. Verify cell_to_coset_evals(coset_evals_to_cell(c)) == c.
3. Generate random valid Cells.
4. Verify coset_evals_to_cell(cell_to_coset_evals(c')) == c'.
5. Test edge cases at field element boundaries.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-2, Trust Boundary: tb-002

checklist_item.title: Does cell_to_coset_evals output exactly FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL elements?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Call cell_to_coset_evals with valid cells.
2. Verify output length == FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_CELL.
3. Test with malformed cells of wrong size.
4. Verify size check prevents FFT dimension mismatch.
5. Test rejection of invalid-sized inputs.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-3, Trust Boundary: tb-002

checklist_item.title: Does _fft_field reject non-power-of-2 input lengths?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Attempt _fft_field with len(vals) not power of 2.
2. Verify rejection before computation.
3. Verify no infinite recursion.
4. Verify no incorrect splits.
5. Test with malformed cells bypassing size validation.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-5, Trust Boundary: tb-002

checklist_item.title: Does inverse FFT apply 1/N normalization factor?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Perform forward FFT on known values.
2. Perform inverse FFT on result.
3. Verify fft(ifft(vals)) == vals.
4. Verify normalization factor 1/N is applied.
5. Test polynomial_eval_to_coeff correctness.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-7, Trust Boundary: tb-003

checklist_item.title: Does coset FFT use non-intersecting evaluation domain?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Compute coset domain points.
2. Compute FFT domain points.
3. Verify no intersection.
4. Verify PRIMITIVE_ROOT_OF_UNITY choice is correct.
5. Test recovery division to ensure no division by zero.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-8, Trust Boundary: tb-008, tb-009

checklist_item.title: Does Fiat-Shamir challenge include ALL verifier-relevant data?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Review hash input construction.
2. Verify domain separator is included.
3. Verify all commitments, indices, evals, proofs included.
4. Attempt to substitute invalid data that passes verification.
5. Identify any omitted fields.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-9, Trust Boundary: tb-006

checklist_item.title: Is RANDOM_CHALLENGE_KZG_CELL_BATCH_DOMAIN globally unique?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Document the domain separator value.
2. Search for collisions with other Ethereum protocols.
3. Search for collisions with external protocols using same hash.
4. Verify uniqueness prevents cross-protocol replay.
5. Document any potential collisions found.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-10, Trust Boundary: tb-006

checklist_item.title: Is Fiat-Shamir hash input order deterministic across clients?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Document canonical serialization order.
2. Compare across multiple client implementations.
3. Verify identical challenge r values for same inputs.
4. Identify any ordering differences.
5. Test batch verification consistency.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-11, Trust Boundary: tb-006

checklist_item.title: Does polynomial_eval_to_coeff require exactly FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB inputs?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Call with correct number of evaluations - verify success.
2. Call with fewer evaluations - verify rejection.
3. Call with more evaluations - verify rejection.
4. Test blob_to_polynomial output count.
5. Verify polynomial degree correctness.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3B4-12, Trust Boundary: tb-002, tb-003

checklist_item.title: Are all KZG proofs generated by compute_cells_and_kzg_proofs_polynomialcoeff valid for their corresponding cells and cosets?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify the SG-019 implementation. 2. For each generated (cell, proof) pair, invoke verify_cell_kzg_proof and confirm True. 3. Test edge cases: zero polynomial, max coefficients, boundary indices. 4. Attempt falsification through subtle corruption. 5. Verify commitment consistency.

checklist_item.title: Does vanishing_polynomialcoeff (SG-012) produce a polynomial that evaluates to zero at all input points with correct degree and monic form?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Identify vanishing_polynomialcoeff (SG-012). 2. Compute vanishing polynomial for test inputs. 3. Verify all evaluations equal zero. 4. Verify degree equals len(xs). 5. Verify leading coefficient is 1.

checklist_item.title: Can construct_vanishing_polynomial be invoked with an empty cell set or missing_cell_indices outside valid range?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Locate construct_vanishing_polynomial (SG-022). 2. Identify call sites and trace data flow. 3. Attempt invalid inputs: empty sets, out-of-range indices. 4. Verify upstream validation rejects these.
